questions.question: Consider a binary classification problem where the prior probability of the positive class (P+) is 0.2 and the likelihood of observing a positive class given a positive instance (L+) is 0.8. Find the posterior probability of the positive class. Show your working and give your answer to 2 decimal places.

A Bayesian classifier uses Bayes' theorem to assign a probability to each class. Assuming equal prior probabilities for both classes, calculate the expected probability of class 2 given an observation that has a probability of 0.3 of belonging to class 1 and a probability of 0.7 of belonging to class 2. Round your answer to 3 decimal places.

A coin is fair, with a probability of 0.5 of landing on heads and a probability of 0.5 of landing on tails. Using Bayesian inference, calculate the posterior probability that a coin is fair given 5 heads and 3 tails. Show your working and give your answer to 4 decimal places.
